raw_consumer_statement:
Dear Dispute Manager, American Express Customer Complaints, I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding my phone interaction with American Express on XXXX XX/XX/year>, when I called to report a fraudulent charge on my AMEX XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

During this call, I was transferred to XXXX XXXX, identified as a XXXX XXXX based in the XXXX. When XXXX XXXX answered the call, there was a baby crying continuously in the background. I asked him to please attend to the baby, as it was very disruptive. There was no apology, nor any explanation. I am extremely concerned as to how an American Express employee can be handling sensitive fraud matters while a baby is present and crying throughout the call. 

I request that American Express review the call recording and confirm whether this is considered acceptable working practice for AMEX staff. 

Following this, I was transferred to XXXX XXXX at the XXXX call center. During this interaction, XXXX XXXX referred to me as a woman, despite the fact that I am a man. I found this disrespectful and unacceptable. When I raised my concern, he refused to take any corrective action and also refused to transfer me to a manager, despite my request.

I have been a loyal American Express customer for over XXXX years, and this experience has been deeply disappointing. The lack of professionalism, incorrect personal address, and refusal to escalate the matter fall far below the standards I expect from American Express. 

I am requesting : A formal apology for the conduct of both representatives Confirmation that the call recordings will be reviewed An explanation of how American Express intends to address this issue and make this right for a long-standing customer Please advise how this matter will be resolved. I expect a prompt and serious response. 

Yours sincerely, XXXX XXXX AMEX XXXX XXXX XXXX
